steps.title: Spot the key signal
steps.content: The text explicitly states that the Octopus Garden contains adults, hatchlings, and eggs, but no juveniles—despite being used exclusively for reproduction.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content: Choice A provides the explanation for this pattern: M. robustus leave upon reaching an intermediary stage of development. The other choices either claim unsupported relationships (B links population stability to temperature variation; C compares eggs across ocean depths) or contradict the text (D calls it a feeding ground, but the site is used exclusively for reproduction). That matches choice A — A is correct.
